sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.93 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a pro-$p$group,$p>2$, and$$an automorphism of$G$of order~2. Suppose that the centralizer$C_G( )$is abelian. Is it true that$G$satisfies a pro-$p$identity? An affirmative answer would generalize a result of A.\,N.\,Zubkov (Siberian Math.~J., 28, no.\,5 (1987), 742--747) saying that non-abelian free pro-$p$groups cannot be represented by$2 2$matrices.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
